evals.prompt: Create a standalone HTML architecture diagram for our ingestion pipeline: API gateway -> queue -> worker -> Postgres. Include flow chips for happy path and retry path, dark mode, clickable nodes, and a side detail panel. Save it as docs/pipeline.html.
evals.expected_output: Uses D2 or another layout renderer for the SVG, then uses the bundled html-diagram wrapper rather than hand-writing a full HTML/CSS/JS shell. Output is one offline HTML file with flow chips and dark mode.

evals.prompt: Turn this service map into a clean local HTML diagram: mobile app calls BFF, BFF calls auth, search, billing, each service emits events to Kafka, analytics consumes Kafka. I need buttons that highlight request flow vs event flow.
evals.expected_output: Creates a graph spec, renders layout with a renderer, prepares stable IDs/data-k values, and wraps the SVG using scripts/html-diagram-wrap.py with a small config JSON.

evals.prompt: Make an HTML diagram comparing current logs pipeline to future harness-neutral traces. It must be reviewable offline, support light/dark themes, and show details when I click a node.
evals.expected_output: Avoids rebuilding the whole HTML shell manually; uses the reusable wrapper, verifies the output file exists, and reports whether browser/screenshot QA was run.
